about.label: About Us
about.title: Research-Based AI Governance for Regulated Environments
about.intro: TrustAI is a research-based initiative from Türkiye, focused on the explainability, reliability, and governance of artificial intelligence systems.
about.sectorFocus: Our current solutions are tailored to the financial sector, where regulatory oversight, transparency, and risk management are of critical importance. In the near future, we plan to expand our services to high-impact domains such as healthcare, manufacturing, and public services.
about.approach: We transform the latest research into practical solutions that are transparent, fair, and audit-ready for high-risk financial applications such as credit risk scoring, fraud detection, and compliance monitoring.
about.team: Our team consists of experts in explainable AI (XAI), optimization, model risk, and MLOps; we combine academic depth with industry experience. We collaborate with financial institutions that value trust, compliance, and resilience.

why.label: Why TrustAI?
why.title: We combine academic rigor with practical governance solutions for high-risk AI systems.

foundation.title: Research-Driven Foundation
foundation.description: Built on peer-reviewed research and academic expertise in explainable and trustworthy AI.

sector.title: Financial Sector Focus
sector.description: Designed specifically for regulated financial environments and high-impact AI applications.

governance.title: Governance by Design
governance.description: Structured workflows, documentation, and traceability embedded from the start.

audit.title: Audit-Ready Architecture
audit.description: Clear model explanations and reporting aligned with regulatory expectations.

hero.title: About TrustAI
hero.subtitle: TrustAI is a research-driven AI governance and trustworthy artificial intelligence initiative, built to enable transparent, auditable, and regulation-ready AI systems in high-risk environments.

whoWeAre.title: Who We Are
whoWeAre.p1: TrustAI is a research-driven AI governance and trustworthy AI initiative based in Türkiye, developed in collaboration with academic institutions and industry partners.
whoWeAre.p2: We focus on explainability, fairness, robustness, and regulatory compliance for high-risk AI systems — particularly in regulated sectors such as finance.
whoWeAre.p3: TrustAI combines academic rigor with production-ready engineering to deliver AI systems that are transparent, auditable, and compliant by design.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.description: To become a leading platform for trustworthy and auditable artificial intelligence, enabling organizations to deploy high-risk AI systems with confidence, transparency, and regulatory alignment.

mission.title: Our Mission
items: Make AI systems explainable and accountable
Reduce model risk in regulated environments
Support compliance with EU AI Act, ISO/IEC 42001, and national AI regulations
Build domestic AI technology with strong scientific foundations

difference.title: What Makes TrustAI Different
difference.p1: TrustAI is not merely a toolkit. It is an integrated governance framework. Unlike fragmented solutions, TrustAI brings together explainability, fairness analysis, robustness monitoring, automated compliance reporting, and MLOps-native architecture — within a unified and structured system.
difference.p2: This integration ensures AI systems are designed to be compliant, transparent, and operationally resilient from inception.

platform.title: TrustAI-X Platform
platform.description: TrustAI-X is our flagship platform — a trustworthy and auditable artificial intelligence infrastructure designed for high-risk environments.

coreCapabilities.title: Core Capabilities
items: SHAP, LIME, and DiCE-Extended counterfactual explanations
EU AI Act compliant technical documentation

scientific.title: Scientific Foundation
scientific.p1: TrustAI is built on peer-reviewed research. Our work includes DiCE-Extended: A Robust Approach to Counterfactual Explanations, presented at MCO 2025 and published by Springer.
scientific.p2: Our research focuses on the stability of counterfactual explanations, multi-criteria optimization, and robust and fair AI decision systems.
scientific.p3: TrustAI aims to produce SCI/SSCI indexed publications, patent applications, graduate-level research outputs, and open-source contributions.

regulatory.title: Regulatory Alignment
regulatory.p1: TrustAI is designed with regulatory compliance embedded from the beginning. Our framework aligns with:
regulatory.p2: Our approach ensures AI systems are documented, traceable, auditable, and continuously monitored.

sectors.title: Target Sectors
sectors.p1: TrustAI primarily focuses on high-risk AI systems in banking, credit scoring, fraud detection, financial risk modeling, and regulated enterprise AI environments.
sectors.p2: The platform is adaptable to manufacturing, public sector, and healthcare domains requiring structured governance frameworks.

team.title: Our Team
team.p1: TrustAI operates through a multidisciplinary structure combining academic leadership, AI and data science researchers, software engineers, MLOps specialists, regulatory experts, and industry advisors.
team.p2: Our work is supported by university R&D infrastructure and research networks, ensuring scientific depth and applied innovation.

closing.statement: AI must be explainable. AI must be measurable. AI must be accountable. AI must be scientifically grounded and regulation-ready.
closing.highlight: TrustAI exists to make that possible.

hero.title: AI Governance: Importance and Impact
hero.subtitle: Artificial intelligence systems increasingly shape high-impact decisions across finance, healthcare, public services, and critical infrastructure. While AI delivers efficiency, scalability, and predictive power, it also introduces risks related to bias, opacity, accountability gaps, and regulatory exposure.

whatIs.title: What is AI Governance?
whatIs.p1: AI governance refers to the structured framework of principles, policies, operational controls, and accountability mechanisms that guide how artificial intelligence systems are designed, deployed, monitored, and corrected.
whatIs.p2: It ensures that AI systems operate in alignment with legal standards, ethical norms, institutional objectives, and long-term societal interests. Governance is not a single compliance checkbox; it is a continuous lifecycle discipline.

bias.title: Bias & Discrimination Risks
bias.description: Without governance, AI models can reinforce historical biases, leading to unfair credit decisions, exclusionary outcomes, or systemic discrimination.

opacity.title: Opacity & Explainability Gaps
opacity.description: Black-box models undermine trust. Governance frameworks require transparent documentation, explainability techniques, and interpretability controls.

regulatory.title: Regulatory & Legal Exposure
regulatory.description: Regulatory environments increasingly demand traceability, auditability, and documented risk controls for AI systems.

accountability.title: Accountability
accountability.description: Clear ownership of model decisions, defined responsibility structures, and escalation mechanisms for high-risk AI systems.

traceability.title: Traceability
traceability.description: Full documentation of model versions, training data sources, validation procedures, and deployment changes across the lifecycle.

monitoring.title: Monitoring & Risk Management
monitoring.description: Continuous oversight of model drift, performance degradation, and emerging compliance risks.

financialSector.title: AI Governance in the Financial Sector
financialSector.p1: In regulated financial environments, AI systems influence credit risk scoring, fraud detection, anti-money laundering processes, and compliance monitoring. These applications directly impact individuals, institutions, and markets.
financialSector.p2: Financial regulators require demonstrable transparency, fairness, and model validation standards. Governance frameworks provide structured workflows, documentation, and reporting mechanisms that ensure models remain defensible and audit-ready.

closing.title: Building Trust Through Structured Governance
closing.description: Effective AI governance transforms advanced machine learning systems into transparent, accountable, and resilient decision infrastructures. It bridges academic research with practical regulatory implementation, ensuring innovation does not compromise fairness, legality, or public trust.

hero.title: Privacy Policy
hero.subtitle: TrustAI values your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your personal information when you visit and interact with our website.

generalPrinciples.title: 1. General Principles
generalPrinciples.p1: We only collect personal data that you provide voluntarily, such as email addresses or contact information submitted through forms or correspondence. We respect your rights to privacy and transparency regarding your personal information.
generalPrinciples.p2: We do not sell, rent, or share your personal data with third parties for marketing purposes. Any sharing of data is limited and governed by legal obligations or trusted service requirements.

whatWeCollect.title: 2. What We Collect
whatWeCollect.intro: When you interact with us, we may collect the following types of information:

items.usage: Usage Data: Browser type, IP address, pages visited, timestamps.
items.communications: Any message content you send through contact forms.

howWeUse.title: 3. How We Use Your Information
items: Provide updates or newsletters if you opt in
Improve the functionality and performance of our services

dataSecurity.title: 4. Data Security
dataSecurity.description: We implement reasonable administrative, technical, and organizational measures to protect your personal data from unauthorized access, alteration, disclosure, or destruction. However, no system is completely secure — absolute protection cannot be guaranteed.

yourRights.title: 5. Your Rights
yourRights.intro: Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have the right to:
items: Request access to the personal data we hold about you
yourRights.contact: To exercise any of these rights, contact us at info{'@'}trustai.com.tr.

cookies.title: 6. Cookies & Tracking
cookies.description: We use cookies and similar technologies to enhance site performance and user experience. You may control cookie settings through your browser preferences. We do not use cookies for advertising tracking.

updates.title: 7. Updates to This Policy
updates.description: This policy may be updated to reflect legal, technological, or business changes. Updated versions will be posted on this page with a revised effective date.
